claims.claim: Putrajaya promised health savings without touching essential services. Salaries are 62% of the budget.
claims.source: MOF press release, 29 April 2026: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/ms/berita/siaran-media/panduan-penjimatan-kerajaan; MOF Budget 2026 B/P.42 PDF, page 407: https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/perbelanjaan/BP.42.pdf; KKM budget guideline updated 22 April 2026, page 10: https://www.moh.gov.my/images/04-penerbitan/garis-panduan-kkm/FINAL%20KSU%20-%20GP%20BELANJAWAN%202026%20-%20KEMASKINI%2022%20APRIL%202026.pdf
claims.correction: Use narrower wording: MOF said expenditure adjustments would not affect critical public services, and MOH said core services should be protected. The 62% figure is accurate if referring to emoluments as a share of the operating allocation: RM24.8415b out of RM39.7757b, or 62.45%. It is not 62% of the total RM46.5202b ministry allocation.
claims.stale_check: No final post-counter-proposal decision was found in the searches through 31 May 2026.

claims.claim: In late April 2026, the Treasury proposed cutting RM3.06 billion (6.6%) from the Health Ministry's RM46.5 billion budget.
claims.source: Bernama, 4 May 2026 (RM3.04b proposed by Treasury Secretary-General): https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2552738; Galen Centre, 29 April 2026 (reported RM3.06b): https://galencentre.org/2026/04/29/proposed-rm3-06-billion-cut-risks-deepening-malaysias-healthcare-crisis/; MOF Budget 2026 B/P.42 PDF confirms RM46.5202156b total: https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/perbelanjaan/BP.42.pdf
claims.correction: Exact official figure found is RM3.04b from Bernama's report of Dzulkefly. RM3.06b is widely reported and equals 6.58% of RM46.5202b, but no public Treasury document publishing the RM3.06b line item was found. Keep as 'reported Treasury proposal of about RM3.04b to RM3.06b' unless the directive is available.
claims.stale_check: Describes the late-April proposal, not a final decision.

claims.claim: The proposed RM3.06 billion cut would leave the Health Ministry RM43.4 billion, below its RM45.3 billion budget the year before.
claims.source: MOF Budget 2026 B/P.42 PDF, page 407 (2025 total RM45.2723728b; 2026 total RM46.5202156b): https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/perbelanjaan/BP.42.pdf; Budget 2026 speech English PDF, para 176 (RM46.5b in 2026, up from RM45.3b): https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/ucapan/bs26.pdf
claims.correction: Arithmetic correct: RM46.5202156b minus RM3.06b equals RM43.46b. Comparison to RM45.3b correct.

claims.claim: The cut was part of a RM10 billion across-the-board savings drive.
claims.source: MOF press release, 29 April 2026 (confirms reprioritisation, not RM10b): https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/ms/berita/siaran-media/panduan-penjimatan-kerajaan; Malaysiakini, 29 April 2026: https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/773928; The Edge, 29 April 2026 (Reuters details): https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/801752
claims.correction: State MOF publicly confirmed a government-wide operating-expenditure reprioritisation, while the RM10b target is reported by media based on the directive/Reuters. Could not verify RM10b from a public MOF document.
claims.stale_check: No newer public MOF document with final savings target found.

claims.claim: The subsidy bill ballooned toward RM58.4 billion from RM15 billion budgeted.
claims.source: Reuters via The Straits Times, 29 April 2026 (RM58.4b projected, above RM15b approved): https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/se-asia/update-2-malaysia-to-slash-2026-federal-operating-spend-over-iran-war-costs; MOF Fiscal Outlook 2026 (subsidies and social assistance projected RM49b): https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/hasil/seksyen3.pdf; MOF press release 29 April 2026: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/ms/berita/siaran-media/panduan-penjimatan-kerajaan
claims.correction: Clarify the denominator. Official Fiscal Outlook 2026 gives RM49b for 'subsidies and social assistance', not RM15b. The RM15b appears in Reuters-based reporting of the internal directive and may refer to a narrower subsidy category (possibly fuel subsidies). Do not write 'the subsidy bill' without defining which category.
claims.stale_check: RM58.4b is a late-April revision driven by the energy shock; may post-date the Budget 2026 fiscal document.

claims.claim: The ministry says it can save without affecting essential services.
claims.source: MOF press release, 29 April 2026 (adjustments without affecting critical public services): https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/ms/berita/siaran-media/panduan-penjimatan-kerajaan; Bernama, 4 May 2026 (MOH requested re-evaluation): https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2552738; The Star, 2 May 2026: https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/02/health-ministry-to-submit-counter-proposal-to-protect-non-negotiable-core-services-from-budget-cut
claims.correction: Better wording: MOF said the reprioritisation should not affect critical public services, while MOH sought re-evaluation and prepared a counter-proposal to protect core services. That is not the same as MOH proving it can absorb the full reduction without affecting essential services.
claims.stale_check: No final acceptance of MOH counter-proposal found.

claims.claim: Of the ministry's RM39.8 billion operating budget, RM24.8 billion (about 62%) is staff salaries.
claims.source: MOF Budget 2026 B/P.42 PDF, page 407 (operating RM39.7757123b; emoluments RM24.8415015b): https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/perbelanjaan/BP.42.pdf; KKM budget guideline 22 April 2026, page 10 (emoluments 62.45% of operating): https://www.moh.gov.my/images/04-penerbitan/garis-panduan-kkm/FINAL%20KSU%20-%20GP%20BELANJAWAN%202026%20-%20KEMASKINI%2022%20APRIL%202026.pdf
claims.correction: Accurate if 'budget' means operating budget. Use 62.45% for precision.
claims.stale_check: Budget 2026 approved figures before the proposed reprioritisation.

claims.claim: Services and supplies make up RM13.9 billion of the rest.
claims.source: MOF Budget 2026 B/P.42 PDF, page 407 (Perkhidmatan dan Bekalan RM13.9312193b): https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/perbelanjaan/BP.42.pdf; KKM budget guideline 22 April 2026 (RM13.9312193b, 35.02%): https://www.moh.gov.my/images/04-penerbitan/garis-panduan-kkm/FINAL%20KSU%20-%20GP%20BELANJAWAN%202026%20-%20KEMASKINI%2022%20APRIL%202026.pdf
claims.correction: Accurate. Services and supplies = RM13.9312b, 35.02% of operating allocation.

claims.claim: A cut cannot realistically reach payroll, so it lands on what is left.
claims.source: MOF Budget 2026 B/P.42 PDF (emoluments largest component): https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/perbelanjaan/BP.42.pdf; The Edge, 29 April 2026 (Reuters: measures included restrictions on salaries/allowances for unfilled vacancies, 10% cut on services/supplies/assets, 20% cut for statutory bodies): https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/801752
claims.correction: Structural inference plausible but overstated. Better: because emoluments are mostly committed payroll, the practical adjustment burden is likely to fall heavily on services, supplies, assets, vacancies, deferred hiring. Do not say payroll cannot be reached at all.

claims.claim: Strip out protected medicine and core care, and the base the cut must fall on is far smaller than the cut itself.
claims.source: The Star, 2 May 2026 (emergency care, medicines, supplies, patient care protected): https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/02/health-ministry-to-submit-counter-proposal-to-protect-non-negotiable-core-services-from-budget-cut; MOF Budget 2026 B/P.42 PDF (aggregate services-and-supplies line, not subcomponents): https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/perbelanjaan/BP.42.pdf
claims.correction: Do not claim the residual base is smaller than the cut unless the analysis quantifies protected subcomponents. Replace with: if core care, medicine supply and medical requirements are protected, the discretionary base becomes materially smaller, but the exact remaining amount is not verified.
claims.stale_check: No final detailed savings schedule found.

claims.claim: Clinicians say the leftover base is not optional. Cleaning prevents infection, maintenance keeps equipment running, and supplies mean medicine, gloves, oxygen, and dialysis lines.
claims.source: Galen Centre, 29 April 2026 (cut felt in wards, theatres, EDs, clinics, pharmacies, labs): https://galencentre.org/2026/04/29/proposed-rm3-06-billion-cut-risks-deepening-malaysias-healthcare-crisis/; CodeBlue, 30 April 2026 (medication stockouts, leaking roofs, dialysis machines): https://codeblue.galencentre.org/2026/04/rm3b-cut-to-health-budget-is-a-prescription-for-crisis-dr-musa-mohd-nordin-prof-emeritus-dr-azizi-omar-prof-dr-zulkifli-ismail/
claims.correction: General attribution supported, but specific examples should be framed as illustrative warnings, not a verified itemised list of budget lines to be cut.
claims.stale_check: No later final savings schedule found.

claims.claim: Doctor groups warned the cut would delay surgeries, lengthen waiting times, and strain staffing.
claims.source: CodeBlue, 30 April 2026 (FPMPAM and MMA): https://codeblue.galencentre.org/2026/04/health-spending-cut-will-worsen-patient-outcomes-strain-health-workers-doctor-groups/; Galen Centre, 29 April 2026: https://galencentre.org/2026/04/29/proposed-rm3-06-billion-cut-risks-deepening-malaysias-healthcare-crisis/
claims.correction: Accurate as attribution to doctor groups. Should not be converted into a claim that harms have already occurred.
claims.stale_check: Warnings made before any final implementation.

claims.claim: The minister has pledged to protect basic care, emergency care, and drug supply.
claims.correction: Accurate, but use 'said' or 'pledged to seek protection for' rather than implying protection was already implemented.

claims.claim: The cut figure is contested: RM3.06b Treasury, RM3.04b minister framing, and about RM4.65b from his 10% expectation.
claims.source: Bernama, 4 May 2026 (RM3.04b by Treasury SG): https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2552738; Galen Centre, 29 April 2026 (RM3.06b): https://galencentre.org/2026/04/29/proposed-rm3-06-billion-cut-risks-deepening-malaysias-healthcare-crisis/; CodeBlue, 13 May 2026 (Dzulkefly expected 10% = RM4.65b): https://codeblue.galencentre.org/2026/05/nothing-in-public-health-care-system-is-a-luxury-kj/
claims.correction: Correctly treat as different reported framings, not one settled number. The 10% calculation is about RM4.65b using RM46.52b.

claims.claim: The cut remained a proposal under re-evaluation following KKM's counter-proposal as of 30 May 2026.
claims.source: Bernama, 4 May 2026: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2552738; The Star, 2 May 2026: https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/02/health-ministry-to-submit-counter-proposal-to-protect-non-negotiable-core-services-from-budget-cut; MOF press release, 29 April 2026: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/ms/berita/siaran-media/panduan-penjimatan-kerajaan
claims.correction: Available public sources support 'proposal under re-evaluation' in early May. No public final decision found by 31 May 2026, but absence of a search result is not proof none exists. Use: 'no public final figure was found in this verification pass'.
claims.stale_check: This is the stale-risk item. Recheck immediately before publication.

claims.claim: A budget that is 62% salaries cannot be trimmed by billions without the cut landing where patients feel it. The honest debate is which harm to accept, not whether harm occurs.
claims.source: MOF Budget 2026 B/P.42 PDF (62.45% emoluments): https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/perbelanjaan/BP.42.pdf; MOF press release, 29 April 2026 (critical services not affected): https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/ms/berita/siaran-media/panduan-penjimatan-kerajaan; CodeBlue/Galen warnings (not proof of harm)
claims.correction: Analytical conclusion stated too strongly. Replace with: Given the fixed-cost structure, a multi-billion reduction would likely place pressure on services, supplies, deferred staffing and other controllable items unless offset by a revised Treasury decision or clearly identified non-critical savings. Health-sector groups warn this could affect patients, but actual harm has not been established.
claims.stale_check: No final implementation or outcome data found.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: A budget that is 62% salaries cannot be trimmed by billions without the cut landing where patients feel it. The honest debate is which harm to accept, not whether harm occurs.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Salary share verified, and health groups warn of impact, but the statement converts a risk and structural inference into a certainty. MOF publicly stated critical public services would not be affected, and no final savings schedule or harm outcome was found.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: MOF press release, 29 April 2026; MOF Budget 2026 B/P.42 PDF

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: as the subsidy bill ballooned toward RM58.4 billion from RM15 billion budgeted
cardinal_sin_flags.why: RM58.4b is from an internal directive, but official Fiscal Outlook 2026 states subsidies and social assistance projected at RM49b. The RM15b comparator needs category clarification; readers may think total budgeted subsidies were RM15b, conflicting with the official RM49b line.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: MOF Fiscal Outlook 2026: https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/hasil/seksyen3.pdf; Reuters via The Straits Times, 29 April 2026

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: The Treasury proposed a RM3.06 billion cut
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Widely reported and close to RM3.04b attributed to the Health Minister by Bernama, but no public Treasury directive or MOF document with the exact RM3.06b found. Keep as 'reported' unless the directive is obtained.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: Bernama, 4 May 2026; MOF press release confirming only general guidance

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: A cut cannot realistically reach payroll, so it lands on what is left.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Structural point plausible, but Reuters-based reporting says measures included restrictions on salaries and allowances for unfilled vacancies. Should not imply payroll-related savings are impossible.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: The Edge Malaysia, 29 April 2026, Reuters-based; MOF Budget 2026 B/P.42 PDF

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Strip out protected medicine and core care, and the base the cut must fall on is far smaller than the cut itself.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Sources verify the aggregate services-and-supplies line and ministerial statements to protect core care, but do not quantify the protected components or prove the residual base is smaller than the proposed cut.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: MOF Budget 2026 B/P.42 PDF; The Star, 2 May 2026

omitted_facts: MOF's public statement on 29 April 2026 confirms only that guidance was issued to reprioritise operating expenditure and says critical public services and economic stability would not be affected. It does not publish the RM3.06b, RM10b or RM58.4b figures. https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/ms/berita/siaran-media/panduan-penjimatan-kerajaan

The official Budget 2026 Fiscal Outlook says subsidies and social assistance were projected at RM49b in 2026. Disclose when using the RM15b comparator from Reuters-based reporting. https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/hasil/seksyen3.pdf

Reuters-based reporting says the directive proposed restrictions on salaries and allowances for unfilled vacancies, a 10% reduction on services/supplies/assets, and a 20% cut on statutory bodies and companies limited by guarantee. https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/801752 and https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/se-asia/update-2-malaysia-to-slash-2026-federal-operating-spend-over-iran-war-costs

Official MOF B/P.42: total MOH allocation RM46.5202156b, operating RM39.7757123b, development RM6.7445033b, emoluments RM24.8415015b, services and supplies RM13.9312193b, 2025 total RM45.2723728b. https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/perbelanjaan/BP.42.pdf

KKM's own budget-management guideline, updated 22 April 2026, states operating allocation RM39.78b and emoluments at 62.45% of operating allocation. https://www.moh.gov.my/images/04-penerbitan/garis-panduan-kkm/FINAL%20KSU%20-%20GP%20BELANJAWAN%202026%20-%20KEMASKINI%2022%20APRIL%202026.pdf
No public final Treasury decision or gazetted revised MOH allocation was found during this verification pass. Re-check immediately before release.
source_assessment: The original analysis cites a reasonable mix of specialist health-policy reporting, mainstream media and official budget data, but relies too heavily on media reports for the most consequential proposal figures. Official budget-composition figures are strong (directly traceable to MOF and KKM documents). Weak points: the exact RM3.06b Treasury line, the RM10b government-wide savings target, and the RM58.4b vs RM15b subsidy comparison, because the public MOF statement confirms the exercise but not those exact numbers. Source diversity moderate: government, Bernama/mainstream, Galen/CodeBlue, doctor groups represented; opposition, patient/community, Treasury document text, parliamentary record, and final implementation documents missing.
overall_assessment: The structural budget arithmetic is mostly reliable: MOH's RM46.52b total allocation, RM39.78b operating allocation, RM24.84b emoluments at 62.45%, RM13.93b services and supplies, and RM45.27b prior-year allocation are verified from official sources. The analysis correctly treats the cut as proposed rather than final. However, several presentation choices overstate certainty: the exact RM3.06b figure is not found in a public Treasury primary source, the RM15b subsidy comparator is ambiguous against MOF's official RM49b subsidies-and-social-assistance figure, and the mature-card conclusion treats likely risk as proven harm. Publish only after tightening those caveats and rechecking whether a final decision was issued after MOH's counter-proposal.
